title: quercus aquatica var. myrtifolia
intro: Quercus aquatica var. myrtifolia is a variety of the water oak tree, native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its smaller leaves and more compact growth habit compared to the typical water oak. The medicinal properties of Quercus aquatica var. myrtifolia include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential in lowering cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Quercus aquatica var. myrtifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice to treat wounds and skin conditions.
